claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. My son Alex is 3 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I understand you have a son named Alex who is 3 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex.

**Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent) towards his care - such as for food, clothes, or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another family member)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete the assessment:

**Does Alex have any special circumstances, such as being in hospital, care, or living away from home for any period of time?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't have any special circumstances like that. He just lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 3 years old (under 16) ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- You are responsible for Alex as he lives with you ✓
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him ✓
- There are no special circumstances affecting his care ✓

You meet all the criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim for Alex.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together in the same home. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict of claims. Alex has no special circumstances such as being in hospital or care that would affect eligibility. Therefore, the claimant meets all the criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 3-year-old son Alex. They meet all the key requirements: they live in the UK, Alex is under 16 years old, they live together (establishing responsibility), no one else is claiming for Alex, and there are no special circumstances that would affect eligibility.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 3 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together in the same home. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict of claims. Alex has no special circumstances such as being in hospital or care that would affect eligibility. Therefore, the claimant meets all the criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 3-year-old son Alex. They meet all the key requirements: they live in the UK, Alex is under 16 years old, they live together (establishing responsibility), no one else is claiming for Alex, and there are no special circumstances that would affect eligibility.
